name: Shangri La
tagline: Doris Duke's oceanfront Islamic art mansion — a jaw-dropping private estate with 2,500 pieces of art from the Islamic world.
parking: Tours depart from Honolulu Museum of Art; shuttle provided
hours: Wed-Sat; tours at set times (must pre-book)
bestTime: Book well in advance; tours are small and sell out
talkStory: Tobacco heiress Doris Duke began building this oceanfront estate in 1937 after her honeymoon travels through the Islamic world. She spent 60 years personally collecting over 2,500 pieces — from Syrian mosaic rooms reassembled tile by tile to Mughal garden pavilions and a 14th-century mihrab from Iran. The swimming pool has an ingenious system that lowers one wall to merge visually with the ocean. Tours are capped at 12 people and depart only from the Honolulu Museum of Art by shuttle — book weeks ahead as they sell out fast. No children under 8 allowed.
